text:
Escalation sweep complete.

**Hot services (4):**
- web-frontend: 8 unresolved Sentry issues
- billing: 7
- api-gateway: 7
- mobile-bff: 7

**PagerDuty incidents acknowledged (5):**
- PD-407 (mobile-bff, high)
- PD-416 (billing, high)
- PD-421 (mobile-bff, low)
- PD-424 (api-gateway, low)
- PD-433 (billing, high)

Email sent to oncall@acme.io with subject "Escalation" and the per-service unresolved counts. No Slack post was made — the "Sentry stable." message is only posted when there are zero hot services.
I'll start by checking the Sentry issues to identify hot services, and also peek at the PagerDuty schema.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT project, COUNT(*) AS unresolved_count
FROM sentry_issues
WHERE status = 'unresolved'
GROUP BY project
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
ORDER BY unresolved_count DESC;"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'pagerduty_incidents';"}

text: 4 hot services found. Let me get the triggered PagerDuty incidents for those services.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, service, urgency FROM pagerduty_incidents WHERE status = 'triggered' AND service IN ('web-frontend','billing','api-gateway','mobile-bff');"}

text: 5 triggered incidents across hot services. Now I'll execute all three actions atomically: acknowledge incidents, send the email, and post to Slack.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"BEGIN;
UPDATE pagerduty_incidents SET status = 'acknowledged'
WHERE status = 'triggered'
  AND service IN ('web-frontend','billing','api-gateway','mobile-bff');
INSERT INTO emails (to_addr, subject, body) VALUES (
  'oncall@acme.io',
  'Escalation',
  'Hot services (>5 unresolved Sentry issues):
- api-gateway: 7
- billing: 7
- mobile-bff: 7
- web-frontend: 8'
);
INSERT INTO sl…(+88)

text: The UPDATE bulk syntax is rejected because the underlying tool requires a specific id. I'll use individual UPDATEs per incident id.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql

text: The slack_messages tool wants a `text` field, not `body`. Let me check the schema.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'slack_messages';"}

text: Right — the column is `text`. Also, the instructions say: "If there are NO hot services, post exactly 'Sentry stable.'". Since there ARE hot services, the Slack post is NOT part of the required flow — it was a stop condition. Re-reading: ho…(+104)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
